generated_oml:
instance TemperatureSensorDS18S20_1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer with -55°C to +125°C range, ±0.5°C accuracy between -10°C to +85°C, unique 64-bit serial code"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureSensorDS18S20_2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer with -55°C to +125°C range, ±0.5°C accuracy between -10°C to +85°C, unique 64-bit serial code"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureSensorDS18S20_3 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer with -55°C to +125°C range, ±0.5°C accuracy between -10°C to +85°C, unique 64-bit serial code"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensorDS18S20_1, TemperatureSensorDS18S20_2, TemperatureSensorDS18S20_3
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Temperature readings from DS18S20 sensors transmitted via RabbitMQ"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ScipyEnabler, PythonEnabler
]

instance Heatbed : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Heatbed with dimensions 214×214×3 mm, controlled via MOSFETs and optocouplers for 4A current handling"
]

instance Fan : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Fan operating at 12V/24V with airflow 0.02 m³/s, actuated through Raspberry Pi GPIO via custom PCB"
]

instance StyrofoamBox : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Styrofoam box with volume 0.03 m³ used for thermal insulation"
]

instance PCBCircuit : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Custom PCB with MOSFETs and optocouplers for high-current driving and electrical safety"
]

instance PowerSupply : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Power supply unit providing 12V/24V for heatbed and fan"
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B controller executing low-level driver and communication tasks"
]

instance ThermalIncubatorSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Thermal incubator system for regulating internal temperature with minimal environmental impact"
    base:contains StyrofoamBox, Heatbed, Fan, TemperatureSensorDS18S20_1, TemperatureSensorDS18S20_2, TemperatureSensorDS18S20_3, RaspberryPiController, PCBCircuit, PowerSupply
]

instance ModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic thermal model with 2 parameters assuming all power energy transfers to air"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonEnabler, ScipyEnabler
]

instance ModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic thermal model with 4 parameters (C_heater, C_air, G_heater, G_box) accounting for heatbed thermal inertia"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonEnabler, ScipyEnabler
]

instance PythonEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python implementation for digital twin components and data processing"
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService, RealTimeMonitoringService, FaultDetectionService, CalibrationService
]

instance ScipyEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Scipy optimization library for non-linear least squares solver in model calibration"
    DTDFVocab:enables CalibrationService
]

instance RabbitMQEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ message broker for communication between physical sensors and digital twin components"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTransmissionService
]

instance GitHubEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "GitHub for open-source repository hosting of digital twin code"
    DTDFVocab:enables CodeRepositoryService
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation models for temperature prediction (Model A and B) providing thermal behavior insights"
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyMonitoringInsight, FaultDetectionInsight, SystemHealthInsight, ControlParameterOptimizationAction
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring for safety compliance using temperature data"
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyMonitoringInsight
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Fault detection via state validation based on model predictions"
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight
]

instance CalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Calibration of physical parameters using experimental data via non-linear least squares solver"
    DTDFVocab:provides CalibratedParametersInsight
]

instance DataTransmissionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Transmission of sensor data via RabbitMQ to digital twin components"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTransmissionInsight
]

instance CodeRepositoryService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Code repository management for digital twin development"
    DTDFVocab:provides CodeVersioningInsight
]

instance SafetyMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of temperature deviations from setpoints for safety compliance"
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Fault detection via state validation based on model predictions"
]

instance SystemHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "System health assessment using real-time model predictions"
]

instance CalibratedParametersInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Calibrated model parameters minimizing residual error between predictions and measurements"
]

instance ControlParameterOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimization of control parameters based on predictive thermal behavior"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataTransmissionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Successful transmission of sensor data to digital twin components"
]

instance CodeVersioningInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Version control and code management for digital twin development"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Controller commands (e.g., heatbed on/off, fan speed control) are sent via RabbitMQ to the low-level driver, which executes actions through the PCB. The bang-bang controller uses parameters LL (lower limit), UL (upper limit), H (heating duration), and C (waiting duration) to regulate temperature based on sensor feedback."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ server on Raspberry Pi coordinates communication between physical components (sensors/actuators via low-level driver) and digital twin components (controller, simulation models). Information flows through message queues with the controller processing sensor data and issuing actuation commands."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin integrates models after system deployment, covering the 'as operated' phase of the physical twin. Models are maintained for ongoing operational analysis rather than discarded post-deployment."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity validated through uniform temperature experiments (three-sensor placement tests) and calibration via least-squares optimization. Model B demonstrated superior accuracy for thermal inertia prediction due to inclusion of heatbed heat accumulation dynamics, with residual error minimized through Scipy optimization."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ message broker used for communication between physical and virtual components. Data transmission occurs via AMQP protocol over local network on Raspberry Pi, with no external network dependencies specified."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "All digital twin components (RabbitMQ server, controller, low-level driver, simulation models) are hosted on Raspberry Pi 4 Model B. The system operates as an embedded solution with no cloud or remote deployment."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Low-level driver implements a safe protocol to maintain device safety. PCB design uses optocouplers for electrical decoupling between control signals and high-current driving circuits, preventing direct Raspberry Pi exposure to hazardous currents."
]
